turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having some issues with my stomach lately. It started about two weeks ago, I'd say - crampy lower abdominal pain, mostly on the left side, and I've been going to the bathroom a lot more often than usual.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The crampy lower abdominal pain is definitely the worst part. It feels like sharp stabbing pains that come and go, but they're really uncomfortable when they hit. The diarrhea is bad too - I'm talking 8 to 10 bowel movements a day, mostly loose or watery with a lot of urgency.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: About two weeks ago, like I said. It just sort of... started out of nowhere.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens every day, all day long - 8 to 10 times a day, to be exact. It's gotten to the point where I'm always on the lookout for a bathroom, and even then, I sometimes make it just in time.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's a solid 9 out of 10. The crampy pain and diarrhea are really impacting my daily life, making it hard for me to work or even leave the house because I'm always worried about when I'll need to go next.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: The fatigue is a big one. I've been feeling exhausted, like I've been running on empty all day. It's affecting my work performance too - I'm normally a software engineer at a startup, but lately, I've just been trying to get through my tasks without burning out. The diarrhea is definitely the worst part, but the fatigue is a close second.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: To be honest, it seems like it gets worse when I'm stressed or anxious, which isn't hard to find these days since I've been working 60+ hour weeks. The diarrhea and cramps seem to intensify when I'm dealing with a lot of pressure at work, but even then, it's not just the stress - it's also the irregular meals and takeout food I've been eating because I don't have time for much else.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: I haven't really noticed any of those symptoms. The only thing that comes close is a low-grade fever - like 37.8 to 38.0 degrees Celsius - but that's been happening a few times a week, not just when I'm experiencing the diarrhea and cramps.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: No, it doesn't really spread. The pain is mostly confined to my lower abdomen, specifically on the left side where it started. Sometimes I feel a bit of discomfort in my lower back, but that's about it.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. To be honest, my main concerns are the crampy pain and diarrhea, which are really impacting my daily life. I'm not sure if swelling would even be noticeable with all that's going on down there.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I've been taking loperamide, also known as Imodium, for about two weeks now to try and control the diarrhea. It hasn't really helped much, to be honest - I'm still having 8-10 bowel movements a day.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. My father has type 2 diabetes, but my mother is healthy and doesn't have any major health issues that I know of.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: I'm not sure about my cholesterol levels - to be honest, I haven't really thought about it until now. Can we focus on the stomach issues first? They're what brought me in today and are causing a lot of distress for me.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I do have one beer most weeknights, but I've recently increased it to 4-5 per night because my symptoms and work stress have been really getting to me. It's not something I'm proud of, but it's become a way for me to cope with everything that's going on.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: To be honest, my diet has been pretty irregular due to my long work hours. I've been relying mostly on takeout and eating at odd times because I don't have a regular schedule. Sometimes I'll skip meals altogether if I'm too busy or stressed. Exercise-wise, I try to get in some sort of physical activity during the week, but it's not like I have a routine going on - more like whatever I can squeeze in when I'm not working non-stop.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: No, not that I know of. My older sister was actually diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, though - we're close, and her health issues have definitely been on my mind lately.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30. That's about it that I can think of, to be honest.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: It's been over two years since I've seen a doctor. To be honest, I'd kind of forgotten how often you're supposed to go in for check-ups and preventative care. With my work schedule being so crazy lately, it just slipped through the cracks, and I haven't prioritized getting back into seeing a doctor until now when these symptoms started showing up.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. To be honest, I'm not even sure if I've ever had an allergic reaction to anything besides maybe some food or drink, but nothing that comes to mind right now is a medication.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: Actually, yes. My weight has been going down lately too - I'd say around 4 kg or 9 pounds over the past three weeks. And it's not just the diarrhea that's causing it, because even when I'm feeling a bit better, I still notice that my appetite isn't what it used to be.
